prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "单腿提踵" (single-leg calf raise on tiptoe) exercise, side view from ground level, the lower body and feet fill the bottom half of the frame. Person posture: Athletic person balanced on the TIPTOES of the RIGHT foot only, like a ballerina en pointe on a single foot. Only the BALL OF THE RIGHT TOES touches the floor; the RIGHT HEEL is lifted HIGH IN THE AIR with a visible gap of about 8-10 cm between heel and floor. The right leg is straight, supporting all body weight. The LEFT LEG is lifted entirely OFF THE GROUND, with the left knee bent at 90 degrees and the left foot tucked behind the right ankle (left foot suspended in air, NEVER touching anything). Left hand placed lightly on a wall to the left for balance. Torso upright. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. EMPHASIS: the figure is balanced on a SINGLE TIPTOE (right toes only), with the right heel dramatically OFF THE FLOOR and the entire left foot in the air.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: calf muscles (gastrocnemius and soleus) of the supporting (right) leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: tibialis posterior and intrinsic foot muscles for stabilization. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腓肠肌" pointing to the gastrocnemius (upper calf); "比目鱼肌" pointing to the soleus (deep calf); "足部稳定肌" pointing to the foot stabilizers. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
